mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This is the official repository for Cluster API.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Cluster API to function.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains all necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components using Helm in the 'capi-system' namespace.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --create-namespace --version v1.12.4

```

> Ensure the namespace is created for the components.
commands: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --create-namespace --version v1.12.4

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource to define a new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureCluster' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n capi-system

```

> You should see all Cluster API components in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this will delete all managed clusters and associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace capi-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This will remove the Cluster API Helm release.
> This will remove the CRDs.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API components
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster resources. This step upgrades the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o yaml > cluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n capi-system

```

> Backup your current cluster resources.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verify that all components are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.4

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD is not installed or not recognized.

**Fix:**
Reapply the Cluster API CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This typically means that the API server is not reachable or the components are not running.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the pods in the capi-system namespace using: kubectl get pods -n capi-system

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: infrastructure provider not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified infrastructure provider is not installed or configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the correct infrastructure provider is installed and configured properly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a problem with the kubeconfig or the API server's certificate.

**Fix:**
Check your kubeconfig file and ensure that the API server's certificate is valid.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for custom infrastructure providers and added new features for cluster scaling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old v1alpha1 API versions for clusters and machines.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to migrate your existing Cluster and Machine resources to the new v1beta1 API.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters' showing your cluster with the status 'Provisioned'.
codeSnippets: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --create-namespace --version v1.12.4

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureCluster
    name: my-infrastructure-cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and have kubectl and helm installed.
